commands.review_summary:
This PR snapshots and restores the per-plugin managed npm project root, including package files, node_modules, and staged npm-pack artifacts, when post-install validation blocks a plugin install or update.

PR surface: Source +254, Tests +252. Total +506 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. with high-confidence source and supplied terminal proof rather than a local rerun. Current main lacks a whole-project snapshot, npm install mutates package files and node_modules, and the PR body shows the before/after blocked-update scenario restoring the tree only with this patch.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is indicated; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the snapshot availability tradeoff plus normal current-head checks before merge.

target.title: Preserve managed npm plugin root when install validation blocks update
target.body:
## Summary
- Snapshot the per-plugin managed npm project root before npm-backed installs mutate it, then restore that project's `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, and `node_modules` when later validation rejects the candidate.
- Stage npm-pack archives inside the managed root only after that rollback snapshot exists, and roll back staged archive artifacts when blocked installs or updates fail.
- Keep managed-root npm config scoping on the real npm install path and request copy-on-write clone copies for snapshot/restore where the filesystem supports them.
- Keep the branch scoped to the plugin installer and focused installer regression tests.

## Behavior
Blocked managed npm installs are not cosmetic failures. npm can already have rewritten the per-plugin managed project before OpenClaw validates the installed candidate. If validation then blocks the install, the old rollback path could leave `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `node_modules`, or staged npm-pack archives in the candidate state.

This PR makes the managed npm install path atomic for the active per-plugin project: failed post-install validation restores the previous project tree, while npm-pack installs also restore or remove the staged tarball used by the temporary `file:` dependency.

The snapshot is scoped to the current per-plugin project under the managed npm root. It does not copy or repair the legacy shared npm root; regression coverage keeps that boundary explicit.

## Scope
- PR branch: `zhuisDEV/codex/preserve-npm-plugin-on-blocked-install`
- Current head: `d8838dbcd2e3e090c37c10a5c8108470a4867a48`
- Current base: `015c6b40aeac14400f6d4fb5f256bac35473958b`
- Changed files: `src/plugins/install.ts`, `src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`
- LOC: GitHub reports `+794/-288` across 2 files; local numstat is `+809/-303`.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: blocked managed npm plugin installs and updates must not uninstall or partially overwrite the active per-plugin project root after npm has already mutated `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `node_modules`, or staged npm-pack archives.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox fresh package install/update runs on Linux with Node `24.15.0`, npm `11.12.1`, and pnpm `11.2.2`, using actual local npm-pack tarballs through the OpenClaw managed npm install path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: built/packed OpenClaw in Crabbox, installed a safe npm-pack plugin version, attempted an unsafe update with install overrides enabled, and compared the managed project tree and staged archive directory after the blocked update; also ran a before/after package-install comparison against the same scenario.
- Evidence after fix: Crabbox terminal output from `run_26e9f9f7591c` (`cbx_37f821f0d935`, `jade-lobster`) and comparison output from `run_620502d429c0` (`cbx_91fff47bec24`, `silver-prawn`):

```text
without fix: avg=5992ms blocked=3/3 tree_restored=0/3 staged_one=0/3 staged_count=2
with fix:    avg=6074ms blocked=3/3 tree_restored=3/3 staged_one=3/3 staged_count=1

RESULT final_fixed=pass blocked=pass tree_restored=pass staged_one=pass exit_code=1 elapsed_ms=5976 staged_count=1
```

- Observed result after fix: blocked installs still returned a failing exit code, but the previous plugin tree was restored every time, exactly one staged archive remained after the safe install, and blocked fresh/update paths did not leave candidate archive debris.
- What was not tested: no published npm registry package was installed during the Crabbox proof; local npm-pack tarballs exercised the same managed npm project install, validation, and rollback path without registry access.

## Validation
- `pnpm format src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts` (`43` tests passed)
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts`
- `git diff --check --cached`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (`autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`)

The final PR cleanup was a base-only rebase after `main` advanced without touching these two files; the installer diff and focused proof remain the same.
target.author: zhuisdev

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
